verses.commentary: No condemnation (katakrima, κατάκριμα) means no judicial verdict of guilt. This is absolute: no condemnation, now or ever. The ground: being in Christ Jesus. Union with Christ changes everything.
The law of sin and death is the principle of sin's deadly dominion (7:23). Liberation comes through Christ Jesus. The Spirit's power breaks sin's power.

God did what the law could not. His own Son emphasizes the cost. Likeness of sinful flesh: Jesus was truly human but without sin. Sin offering (peri hamartias, περὶ ἁμαρτίας) echoes Leviticus. God condemned sin in the flesh through Christ's death. The penalty was paid in Christ's body.
Christ met the law's demands. We live according to the Spirit, not the flesh. The Spirit enables what the law commanded but could not produce.
The mind's focus reveals the heart's allegiance. Flesh-minded people pursue fleshly things; Spirit-led people pursue spiritual things.
The mind (phronēma, φρόνημα, mindset, way of thinking) determines destiny. Flesh produces death; Spirit produces life and peace (shalom, wholeness).
It will not and cannot submit. This is total inability. Apart from the Spirit, submission to God is impossible.
Those dominated by the flesh (unregenerate) cannot please God. Whatever they do, apart from faith and the Spirit, falls short. Pleasing God requires regeneration.

If introduces not doubt but the condition: having the Spirit. The Spirit's indwelling is the mark of belonging to Christ. No Spirit, no Christianity. The Spirit of God and Spirit of Christ are used interchangeably, affirming the Trinity.
The body still dies (physical death is the result of sin). But the Spirit gives life: spiritual life now, resurrection life later. Righteousness is the ground of the Spirit's life-giving work.
Resurrection is guaranteed by the indwelling Spirit. Mortal bodies will be made immortal. The Spirit is the firstfruits (v. 23) and guarantee of final redemption.
We owe the flesh nothing. It enslaved us; Christ freed us. Obligation is to the Spirit, not the flesh.

Living according to the flesh leads to spiritual death. Putting to death the deeds of the body is mortification: active killing of sin. This is done by the Spirit, not human effort. The result is life.
This is not sinless perfection but a pattern of life. Children of God (huioi theou, υἱοἱ θεοῦ) emphasizes sonship, not just creation.

Adoption (huiothesia, υἱοθεσία) confers full legal rights as sons. Abba (Aramaic) and Father (Greek) together express intimate relationship. Crying Abba is the Spirit's testimony in us. We are children, not slaves.
This is internal assurance. The Spirit confirms what God declares. Our spirit and the Spirit bear joint testimony. This is the ground of Christian assurance.

Sonship means heirship. We inherit with Christ. Co-heirs (synklēronomoi, συγκληρονόμοι) means equal inheritance. Suffering precedes glory. Sharing Christ's sufferings qualifies us to share His glory. This is the already-not yet: suffering now, glory later.
Glory will be revealed in us, not just to us. We will be glorified, not just spectators of glory.
Eager expectation (apokaradokia, ἀποκαραδοκία) suggests intense longing, head outstretched. Creation longs for the revelation of God's sons because it will share in their liberation.

Frustration (mataiotēs, ματαιότης) means futility, emptiness. Creation was subjected to this by God (consequence of Adam's sin, Genesis 3:17-18). But it was subjected in hope. The curse is not permanent.
It will share in the glorious freedom of God's children. New heavens and new earth (Revelation 21:1) fulfill this promise. Redemption is cosmic, not just personal.
Childbirth pains imply something new is coming. The groaning is purposeful, not meaningless. Creation travails, awaiting redemption.

The firstfruits of the Spirit is the down payment, guaranteeing full harvest. We groan for full adoption: bodily resurrection. Redemption includes the body, not escape from it. We await physical transformation.
We are saved (past), but full salvation is future. Hope is confident expectation of what is not yet seen. Seeing eliminates the need for hope.
Waiting is active, not passive. We endure present suffering in light of future glory.
We don't know how to pray as we should. The Spirit intercedes with groans too deep for words. The Spirit prays in us and for us.
The Spirit's intercession aligns with God's will. This guarantees that our prayers, even when inarticulate, are heard and answered according to God's perfect plan.

Not that all things are good, but God uses all things for good. The beneficiaries: those who love God and are called. Called implies divine initiative. Good is defined by God's purpose, not our comfort.
Predestination is God's determination of the end: conformity to Christ's image. The goal: Christ as firstborn among many siblings. We become like Jesus.

Past tense throughout, even glorified (already accomplished in God's decree). The order: predestination, calling, justification, glorification. Nothing breaks the chain. Those God predestines are certainly glorified.
If God is for us, all opposition is overcome. This is not that no one opposes, but that no opposition succeeds. God's favor guarantees victory.

The argument from greater to lesser. If God gave His Son (the greatest gift), He will give everything else. Graciously emphasizes grace. All things means everything needed for salvation and godliness.
Who can accuse? No one, because God justifies. If the Judge declares righteous, no accusation stands. God's verdict silences all accusers.

Who condemns? No one. Christ died (atonement), was raised (vindication), is at God's right hand (exaltation), and intercedes for us (ongoing advocacy). Our defense lawyer is the Judge's Son. Condemnation is impossible.
Can any of these separate us from Christ's love? The implied answer: No. External circumstances cannot sever the love bond.
Suffering is expected, not surprising. Sheep to be slaughtered depicts vulnerability. Yet even in this, we are not abandoned.
We don't just survive; we triumph. Victory comes through Him who loved us. Christ's love is the ground of our conquest.
A comprehensive list: death, life, angels, demons, present, future, powers. Nothing in existence can separate.

Absolutely nothing can separate us from God's love in Christ Jesus. This is the believer's unshakable security. The love of God is in Christ Jesus our Lord. Union with Christ guarantees eternal union with God's love.
